metadata.author: David Ancor
metadata.stakeholders: Founders; Product Managers; Engineering Teams; Team Admins; Compliance Officers; Site Reliability / DevOps
metadata.parent_okr: Ship c1v v1.0 with metric-traceable specs and live feedback loop
metadata.regulatory_refs: SOC 2 Type II; HIPAA; GDPR; PCI-DSS (scope tbd in Module 4)

initial_conditions: A spec is live (approved by UC03 and emitted to production via UC04).
A non-invasive read-only connection to Observability Tools exists (established by UC11).
The system has ingested metric samples over a sustained window of at least AGGREGATION_WINDOW_MIN.
At least one LLM Provider is configured for the organization.
The Engineering Team Member is authenticated and subscribed to the project's recommendation stream.

actor_steps_table.other_actors: Observability Tools stream availability, throughput, and latency samples into the feedback-loop ingest (<<include>> UC05).

actor_steps_table.the_system: The system shall persist every ingested metric sample to the feedback-loop store with the source observability identifier and a monotonic ingest timestamp.
The system shall bound the overhead of metric ingestion on the customer system to at most MAX_CUSTOMER_SYSTEM_OVERHEAD_PCT of the customer system's measured baseline resources.
The system shall aggregate persisted samples over the AGGREGATION_WINDOW_MIN window on a RECOMMENDATION_CADENCE_MIN schedule.
The system shall compare every aggregate against the spec's declared performance targets for availability, throughput, and latency.
The system shall compute a metric deviation value for every aggregate that falls outside the spec's declared target.
The system shall suppress deviations whose magnitude is below DEVIATION_SUPPRESSION_THRESHOLD to prevent noise surfacing.
The system shall query a configured LLM Provider with each unsuppressed deviation to propose candidate improvements.
The system shall link every candidate improvement to the deviating metric and its source sample identifiers.
The system shall rank candidate improvements by deviation severity and LLM-reported confidence within RECOMMENDATION_LATENCY_SEC of the cycle start.
The system shall cap the delivered recommendation list at MAX_RECOMMENDATIONS_PER_CYCLE entries.
The system shall deliver the ranked recommendation list to the Engineering Team Member's recommendation stream.

actor_steps_table.primary_actor: Engineering Team Member receives the recommendation list

actor_steps_table.extra_actor_col: Product Managers receive a digest of the recommendation cycle.

actor_steps_table.the_system: The system shall notify Product Managers subscribed to the project of the recommendation cycle completion.
The system shall record an audit entry for the recommendation cycle with the window boundaries, sample count, deviation count, recommendation count, and timestamp (CC.R02).

actor_steps_table.primary_actor: Engineering Team Member selects an action per recommendation: accept, decline, or defer

actor_steps_table.the_system: The system shall persist the Engineering Team Member's action per recommendation with actor identity and timestamp.
The system shall surface a proactive feature candidate when a deviation indicates missing functionality (<<extends>> UC07).
The system shall refrain from writing any recommendation back to the customer system in v1 scope (read-only posture).

ending_conditions: A ranked recommendation list bounded by MAX_RECOMMENDATIONS_PER_CYCLE is delivered to the Engineering Team Member's recommendation stream.
Every delivered recommendation is linked to its source metric deviation and source sample identifiers.
Product Managers subscribed to the project have received a recommendation cycle digest.
Every recommendation carries a persisted action state of accepted, declined, or deferred once the Engineering Team Member acts.
An audit entry records the recommendation cycle with window boundaries, counts, and timestamp.
No recommendation is written back to the customer system (read-only posture preserved in v1).
notes: Scope boundary (v1 read-only): UC06 delivers recommendations; it does NOT apply them. Write-through (suggest-and-apply) is explicitly deferred to v1.1+ per M1 open-question resolution.
<<include>> UC05 on step 1 — metric ingestion is the upstream leg, treated here as a single boundary step.
<<extends>> UC07 on step 19 — proactive feature surfacing branches off when a deviation indicates missing functionality; a separate flow in v1.1 converts that candidate into a PM backlog item.
Non-invasive overhead: step 3 enforces the M1 hard constraint MAX_CUSTOMER_SYSTEM_OVERHEAD_PCT on ingestion.
Cadence discipline: RECOMMENDATION_CADENCE_MIN governs how often cycles run; AGGREGATION_WINDOW_MIN governs the sample window each cycle evaluates.
Deviation suppression (step 7) prevents alert fatigue on sub-threshold noise; threshold is tuned per project.
Latency: RECOMMENDATION_LATENCY_SEC bounds step 4→10 end-to-end; this is a batch latency, not a per-sample latency.
Audit trail (step 16) captures the cycle as a single event; per-recommendation actions (step 18) produce finer-grained audit rows.
Degradation: if all configured LLM Providers are unavailable, the system shall defer the cycle and emit an operational alert; no silent drop of deviations.

extends_from_elsewhere: UC07 extends UC06 when a deviation implies missing functionality
